selection_rules: grammar must not require external scanners for its primary parse path
grammar should avoid depending on runtime surfaces that the repo does not yet claim
grammar should be small enough to keep harness iteration cheap
the shortlist should include more than one grammar style
disqualification_rules: requires external scanner support for the normal parse path
requires broader lexer or scanner parity than the current staged surface
depends on runtime or ABI surfaces explicitly deferred beyond current milestones
is too large or brittle to serve as a stable first-wave harness target

targets.success_means: load, prepare, emit all parser surfaces, pass compat-check, and compile emitted parser.c

targets.why: richer scanner-free grammar with nested structure
targets.success_means: load, prepare, emit all parser surfaces, pass compat-check, and compile emitted parser.c

targets.why: JS-path target that still fits the parser-only staged boundary
targets.success_means: load through node, emit parser surfaces, and preserve the staged blocked boundary without infrastructure failure

targets.why: parser-only ambiguity case that is useful for later mismatch expansion
targets.success_means: classify the unresolved conflict boundary explicitly before promoting it into the first-wave run set

targets.why: requires external scanner handling beyond the current parser-only boundary
targets.success_means: remain explicitly excluded until external-scanner coverage is in scope
